extraction.one_line_summary: SAGAN Peter won the uphill sprint finish after BORA-hansgrohe controlled the circuit lap finale, taking 10 bonus seconds to move fifth overall.
tactical_patterns: BORA-hansgrohe paced entire circuit to neutralize breakaway threats before uphill sprint, sacrificing stage hunters for SAGAN Peter positioning.
DENNIS Rohan group maintained 9-second advantage despite repeated circuit climbs, suggesting prologue gaps survived selective terrain surprisingly well.
PASQUALON Andrea attacked inside 1km at roundabout before finish but failed when SAGAN Peter waited, revealing sprint timing advantage.

rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: positive: won uphill finish after eight circuit climbs, explicitly targeting Giro preparation rather than protecting GC position
rider_signals.COLBRELLI Sonny: negative: finished second despite BAHRAIN team control work, frustrated body language suggests uphill gradient too steep for pure speed
rider_signals.BAUHAUS Phil: negative: absent from finale group, confirms cannot handle repeated 4x circuit climbs despite sprint capability
rider_signals.PASQUALON Andrea: neutral: late attack showed positioning but lacked power to hold off SAGAN Peter on gradient
rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: positive: retained 9-second lead through selective circuit, INEOS depth suggests time trial specialists can survive punchy stages with team support
